symbols: white lilies — the purity and virginity of Mary, the most traditional symbol of the Annunciation
enclosed garden — the hortus conclusus of Song of Solomon 4:12, a type of Mary's perpetual virginity
roses among flowers — love and the mystical rose, a Marian title (Rosa Mystica)
angel appearing at garden gate — the divine messenger entering the human realm, heaven touching earth
Mary's white garment — purity, innocence, and receptivity to God's will
red brick house behind Mary — domestic setting emphasizing the Incarnation entering ordinary human life
lush verdant garden — the new Eden where the new Eve receives the Word and reverses the Fall
description: A garden scene depicting the Annunciation in a naturalistic English cottage setting. At left, a figure in a pinkish-red robe appears at the entrance of the garden, partially framed by greenery and a wooden gate — this is the angel. At right, a young woman in a flowing white dress turns with an expression of surprise, one hand raised. Between them, a magnificent display of tall white Madonna lilies blooms abundantly. The garden is lush with roses, orange flowers, and dense green foliage. A red brick building is visible behind the woman. The scene is bathed in gentle, natural light.
theological_description: This Annunciation transposes the biblical scene into an English garden, yet preserves its core theological meaning with remarkable precision through the language of flowers. The profusion of white lilies — the traditional Marian symbol of purity — forms the visual center, connecting the angel's message to Mary's virginal motherhood. The enclosed garden (hortus conclusus) has been a Marian type since patristic exegesis of Song of Solomon 4:12: 'A garden enclosed is my sister, my bride.' The angel's arrival disrupts Mary's ordinary world, just as the Incarnation disrupts all of human history. Luke 1:28-38 records the exchange: the angel's greeting (Ave gratia plena), Mary's troubled questioning, and her fiat (let it be done to me according to your word). This moment is the hinge of salvation history — the new Eve's obedience reverses the old Eve's disobedience (Irenaeus, Against Heresies III.22). The domestic garden setting emphasizes the scandal of particularity: the infinite God enters creation through one woman's yes, in one specific place and moment, transforming the ordinary into the threshold of the divine.

scripture_refs.reason: The angel Gabriel is sent to Mary in Nazareth and announces the conception of Jesus — the scene depicted
Hail, full of grace, the Lord is with you — the moment of angelic greeting captured in the painting
The virgin shall conceive and bear a son — the Isaianic prophecy fulfilled at the Annunciation
A garden enclosed is my sister, my bride — the enclosed garden as a type of Mary's virginity
The seed of the woman shall crush the serpent — Mary as the new Eve through whom the promise is fulfilled
When the fullness of time had come, God sent His Son, born of a woman — the Annunciation as that moment
